cve.title: CVE-2026-48829 - Apache GNU SASL NULL Pointer Dereference Vulnerability
cve.contentSnippet: In GNU SASL before 2.2.3, DIGEST-MD5 has a NULL pointer dereference affecting both clients and servers, via a known token with no accompanying = character. This occurs in lib/digest-md5/getsubopt.c.

ai.title: Catch up on the Dialogues stage at Google I/O 2026.
ai.contentSnippet: A recap of the 2026 I/O Dialogues, where leaders discuss the future of AI, quantum computing, robotics and creativity.

ai.title: Amazon Nova Act is now HIPAA eligible
ai.contentSnippet: In this post, you will learn what Nova Act offers, how HIPAA eligibility applies to agentic AI, and how to get started.

ai.title: Datasette Agent

system.title: A large set of stable kernel updates
system.contentSnippet: The 7.0.10, 6.18.33, 6.12.91, 6.6.141, 6.1.174, 5.15.208, and 5.10.257 stable kernel updates have all been released. The first four are huge (the 7.0.10 review version had 1,146 commits) while 6.1.174, 5.15.208, and 5.10.257 are small updates for the "Fragnesia" vulnerability.

system.title: [$] Custom page-cache policies with BPF
system.contentSnippet: The kernel's page cache is charged with maintaining pages (or, more correctly, folios) containing copies of data from files in the filesystem; its performance has a big effect on the performance of the system as a whole. One of the key decisions the kernel must make is when to ev

system.title: [$] Toward better handling of major page faults
system.contentSnippet: A major page fault occurs when a process attempts to access a page that is not currently present in RAM; satisfying such faults usually involves I/O, and can thus take some time. When many threads sharing an address space are generating page faults, the result can be significant
